programme.title: One Strange Rock
programme.desc: A story about the creation and history of the planet, the laws governing it, its shape, the richness of flora and fauna, threats to the natural environment, natural wonders and surprising relationships.Actor Will Smith and people who have had the opportunity to spend time in space will talk about this extraordinary place in the universe.Accounts from astronauts including Mae Jemison, Mike Massimino, Leland Melvin and Chris Austin Hadfield will provide a completely new perspective on Earth.Director Darren Aronofsky and his film crew visited 45 countries and six continents.The photos were taken, among others, in the Arctic, the Himalayas, India and during the annual festival in Tarragona, Spain.Materials recorded on board the International Space Station were also used.In the following episodes, viewers will learn how oxygen is produced, the protective layers of the Blue Planet and the entire sequence of events that led to the creation of life.The topic of death, colonization, destruction, the search for alien forms and human intellect will also be discussed.

programme.title: Savage Kingdom: Uprising
programme.desc: The film is dedicated to a lion named Sekekama.He is the undisputed leader of the pack.However, to maintain power, he must defend his throne against his rivals.These are three eastern princes who want to rule the entire territory.They are ready to overthrow the current king, and each of them may pose a real threat to his position.A new, powerful empire is also created by the dangerous Queens of the North.Three lionesses were once expelled from the pride, and now they tirelessly plan bloody revenge on their enemies.Meanwhile, the lioness Saba struggles with a personal tragedy.At the same time, she constantly works to ensure that her only son follows the path she has set out for her.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol 9
programme.sub-title: Who caught up with the dog
programme.desc: Dr. Pol has been treating animals for many years.He saved the life of more than one animal.He is helped every day by his colleagues who are willing to make sacrifices.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol 9
programme.desc: Dr. Pol has been treating animals for many years.He saved the life of more than one animal.He is helped every day by his colleagues who are willing to make sacrifices.

programme.title: Incredible Dr. Pol 10
programme.desc: Another season of hard work by Dr. Jan Pol and his colleagues.The team will need a new member because Dr. Emily is expecting a baby and someone will have to replace her.

programme.title: Europe's Wild Places Mashup
programme.desc: We set off on a journey along the wild trails of Europe.We will see unique corners of the continent full of natural beauty, and at the same time meet its peculiar inhabitants.

programme.title: America The Beautiful: Wild Frontier
programme.desc: A documentary series devoted to the American border and its nature.The program presents the legends of the Wild West, which are intertwined with stories about local animal species.

programme.title: Critter Fixers: Country Vet: Appreciation Compilation 2023
Critter Fixers: Country Vets: Compilation Season 2
Secrets of the Penguins
programme.desc: For two years, National Geographic explorer Bertie Gregory closely studied the tight-knit communities of some of the world's most intelligent penguins.He documented behaviors and situations that had previously been difficult to capture on camera.The series proves that penguins are much closer to humans than was previously known.These unique flightless birds are brave and adventurous, and at the same time value family bonds and friendship.Thanks to their ingenuity, they can survive in the most extreme conditions Millions of years ago, a group of penguins left the icy lands, using powerful ocean currents that carried them to distant lands.Over time, these birds adapted to living in completely different places - from deserts, through the tropics, to areas inhabited by people.The fight against adversity made them one of the most intelligent birds in the world.National Geographic explorer Bertie Gregory examines their problem-solving skills, communication methods, and motivation to explore new environments.

programme.title: Secrets of the Octopus
programme.desc: We discover eight different species of octopuses in the depths of the sea.Each has a unique personality and great intelligence.Despite drastic differences, these animals turn out to be surprisingly similar to humans.

programme.title: Last Rhinos, The: A New Hope
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the unique mission to save the northern rhinoceros.There are only two females left in the world.Scientists intend to achieve the first-ever surrogate pregnancy in a rhinoceros.

programme.title: Africa's Deadliest: Compilation Season 5
programme.desc: The animals observed in the program are unrivaled hunting masters.They are fast and deadly.They evolved in the harsh African environment where only the strongest individuals can survive.

programme.title: Snakes in the City: Uncharted Compilation 6
Europe From Above 5
programme.desc: The Old Continent seen from an extraordinary perspective.A bird's eye view will allow you to discover architectural treasures as well as natural gems.Europe will also be shown during different seasons.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol 9
programme.desc: Dr. Pol has been treating animals for many years.He saved the life of more than one animal.He is helped every day by his colleagues who are willing to make sacrifices.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol 9
programme.desc: Dr. Pol has been treating animals for many years.He saved the life of more than one animal.He is helped every day by his colleagues who are willing to make sacrifices.

programme.title: Secrets of the Penguins
programme.desc: For two years, National Geographic explorer Bertie Gregory closely studied the tight-knit communities of some of the world's most intelligent penguins.He documented behaviors and situations that had previously been difficult to capture on camera.The series proves that penguins are much closer to humans than was previously known.These unique flightless birds are brave and adventurous, and at the same time value family bonds and friendship.Thanks to their ingenuity, they can survive in the most extreme conditions Millions of years ago, a group of penguins left the icy lands, using powerful ocean currents that carried them to distant lands.Over time, these birds adapted to living in completely different places - from deserts, through the tropics, to areas inhabited by people.The fight against adversity made them one of the most intelligent birds in the world.National Geographic explorer Bertie Gregory examines their problem-solving skills, communication methods, and motivation to explore new environments.

programme.title: Secrets of the Octopus
programme.desc: We discover eight different species of octopuses in the depths of the sea.Each has a unique personality and great intelligence.Despite drastic differences, these animals turn out to be surprisingly similar to humans.

programme.title: Critter Fixers: Country Vet: Appreciation Compilation 2023
Something Bit Me! Compilation Season
programme.desc: The creators of the program will talk about the most terrifying animal attacks on people.The characters in subsequent episodes needed medical help due to stings, bites, infections and wounds, often life-threatening.Viewers will learn, among others:a waitress who encounters the world's largest predator on her way home, and a surfer who returned to the shore with a strange bite.In addition to the accounts of the victims, they will also hear comments from experts who will talk about the nature of wild animals and the causes of their aggression.They will also provide a scientific analysis of the facts, helping you understand the motivations of the attacking creatures.

programme.title: Something Bit Me! Compilation Season
programme.desc: The creators of the program will talk about the most terrifying animal attacks on people.The characters in subsequent episodes needed medical help due to stings, bites, infections and wounds, often life-threatening.Viewers will learn, among others:a waitress who encounters the world's largest predator on her way home, and a surfer who returned to the shore with a strange bite.In addition to the accounts of the victims, they will also hear comments from experts who will talk about the nature of wild animals and the causes of their aggression.They will also provide a scientific analysis of the facts, helping you understand the motivations of the attacking creatures.

programme.title: Girl Power
programme.desc: In the animal kingdom, females often lead the herd, hunt, obtain food, and decide on the choice of partner.They know the applicable rules, and on the other hand, they often create them themselves.

programme.title: Underdogs
programme.desc: The creators of the National Geographic documentary series set themselves the goal of showing the natural world from a new perspective.They invited actor Ryan Reynolds to cooperate, who is the narrator in the original language version.This extraordinary story will not include heroes such as fast cheetahs, brave lions or eagle-eyed birds of prey.The main role will be played by animals that seem to be failures.It turns out that their weaknesses and apparent defects have enormous potential.Stunning wildlife photography and music by Harry Gregson-Williams ("Shrek," "The Martian") set the backdrop to the adventures of freaks and outcasts of the fauna kingdom who have developed effective survival techniques, resilience and surprising instincts.Among them is the pistol shrimp, which can shoot hot water bubbles.Some species of frogs have superpowers - they become invisible.The anglerfish, on the other hand, is a master of camouflage.The next episodes will feature terrible parents and poor seducers.Cameras will follow the peculiarities of the world of meerkats, sloths, macaques, proboscis monkeys, mosquitoes and snails.There will be plenty of humor and surprising plot twists.

programme.title: Wild Alaska
programme.desc: The rugged coastline of the largest state in the United States is home to strange species.Winter has just ended.Now animals can enjoy longer days.

programme.title: Secrets of The Zoo: The Wild Side
Primate Planet
programme.desc: The creators will introduce the world of primates, taking viewers from the crowns of tropical trees to snow-capped peaks.They will show the extraordinary behaviors of these animals, their strong family bonds and unique personalities.

programme.title: Untamed Americas
programme.desc: The territory of North and South America stretches over 15,000 kilometers - from the Arctic Archipelago to Tierra del Fuego.No other continent has such breadth and diversity of landscape.Here you can admire the longest mountain chain in the world - the Andes, the Amazon rainforest and the driest area on Earth - the Atacama Desert.Unique species of animals live in these wild areas.The authors hunt grizzly bears, encounter bats with extremely long tongues, jaguars attacking caimans and dolphins swimming among the trees.From cold Alaska, through the tropical Caribbean and the green Amazon - famous actor Josh Brolin takes viewers on this amazing journey.

programme.title: Moody Beasts
programme.desc: Viewers will find answers to questions about animals.They will learn why male moose become covered in their own urine, clownfish change sex, and the brown moose dies after mating.

programme.title: Close Encounters of the Wild Kind: Season 1
One Strange Rock
programme.desc: A story about the creation and history of the planet, the laws governing it, its shape, the richness of flora and fauna, threats to the natural environment, natural wonders and surprising relationships.Actor Will Smith and people who have had the opportunity to spend time in space will talk about this extraordinary place in the universe.Accounts from astronauts including Mae Jemison, Mike Massimino, Leland Melvin and Chris Austin Hadfield will provide a completely new perspective on Earth.Director Darren Aronofsky and his film crew visited 45 countries and six continents.The photos were taken, among others, in the Arctic, the Himalayas, India and during the annual festival in Tarragona, Spain.Materials recorded on board the International Space Station were also used.In the following episodes, viewers will learn how oxygen is produced, the protective layers of the Blue Planet and the entire sequence of events that led to the creation of life.The topic of death, colonization, destruction, the search for alien forms and human intellect will also be discussed Life on Earth exists thanks to the atmosphere, which appears to people in space as a thin blue layer.An extraordinary chain of relationships leads to the production of oxygen, the most abundant element on the planet.Disrupting the delicate balance can leave living creatures unable to breathe.The creators of the series will talk about surprising phenomena - a sandstorm covering almost the entire world, the Amazon and its sky rivers, the power hidden by glaciers and single-celled algae - diatoms.

programme.title: Europe From Above 5
programme.desc: The Old Continent seen from an extraordinary perspective.A bird's eye view will allow you to discover architectural treasures as well as natural gems.Europe will also be shown during different seasons.
